title: aes/ecc/rsa: tag/output length passed as magic literal instead of typed constant
description: In crypto/aes.rs gcm_encrypt, the AES-GCM tag size is passed to the C shim as the bare integer 16 (wolfhsm_aes_gcm_encrypt(..., tag.as_mut_ptr(), 16)). The tag buffer is [u8; 16], so tag.len() is 16 by construction — but the reader has to cross-reference the declaration to confirm the literal matches. In crypto/ecc.rs public_key_der, the capacity is the magic literal 91. In both cases the literal is correct but the connection to the type is implicit. Rewrite: derive lengths from the buffer: tag.len() as u32 / 32 for the tag, and introduce a named constant P256_SPKI_DER_LEN: u32 = 91 for the ECC public key buffer.
